file_insights.purpose: Main Docker Compose configuration for local development
notable_patterns: Uses service_healthy condition for dependency management
Implements health checks for all services

key_components: Redis Commander cache management UI (optional profile)
notable_patterns: Uses profiles for optional services (mail, tools)

file_insights.purpose: PostgreSQL initialization script for container startup
notable_patterns: Uses CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency
Turkish language support for menu search

patterns_discovered.description: Uses Dockerfile targets (development, celery-worker, celery-beat) for different service types
patterns_discovered.benefit: Optimized images for different purposes, reduced image sizes

patterns_discovered.description: All core services implement health checks (PostgreSQL, Redis, Django web)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures services are ready before dependent services start, improves reliability

patterns_discovered.description: Extensive use of ${VAR_NAME:-default_value} syntax for all settings
patterns_discovered.benefit: Single compose file works across environments with different .env files

patterns_discovered.description: Named volumes for persistence, delegated/cached mounts for performance optimization
patterns_discovered.benefit: Data persistence, improved performance, clear volume organization

patterns_discovered.description: Uses Docker Compose profiles (mail, tools) for optional services like MailHog, pgAdmin, Redis Commander
patterns_discovered.benefit: Lean default setup with optional tools available on demand

patterns_discovered.description: Extensive comments and usage instructions in compose files
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear development experience for team members

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Service startup timing with wait-for-it patterns
gotchas_discovered.description: Development setup includes sleep and dependency checks in web service command; may need refinement for faster startup
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Uses depends_on with service_healthy conditions and 2-second sleep buffer

gotchas_discovered.description: MailHog is optional (profile: mail) and not enabled by default; developers must explicitly enable it
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear documentation and optional profile pattern; can be enabled with COMPOSE_FILE env variable

gotchas_discovered.description: SQL initialization script mounted as read-only, must be complete; any schema changes require container recreation
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Django migrations handle most changes, but pure SQL changes need volume cleanup

gotchas_discovered.description: Redis has 256mb maxmemory limit with allkeys-lru policy; may need adjustment for heavy local development
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Configurable via command line, but hardcoded in main compose file (should be in .env)

gotchas_discovered.description: Django web service has 60-second start period, may timeout during slow builds or migrations
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documented delay with echo messages in startup command

approach_outcome.description: Complete Docker Compose setup for local development with PostgreSQL, Redis, Django, and Celery services
key_achievements: Production-ready configuration with proper health checks and service dependencies
Comprehensive development overrides with hot-reload and debugging tools
Optional services (MailHog, pgAdmin, Redis Commander) via profiles
Database initialization script with PostgreSQL extensions
Clear volume management strategy for data persistence and performance

recommendations.recommendation: Move hardcoded Redis memory limits to environment variables
recommendations.rationale: Redis maxmemory and policy are hardcoded in command; should be configurable via .env for flexibility
recommendations.suggested_change: Extract --maxmemory and --maxmemory-policy to REDIS_MAXMEMORY and REDIS_MAXMEMORY_POLICY env vars

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit wait-for-it script instead of sleep
recommendations.rationale: Current approach uses arbitrary 2-second sleep; dedicated wait script would be more reliable
recommendations.suggested_change: Implement or use existing wait-for-it.sh script for service readiness checks

recommendations.recommendation: Document required .env variables with example .env.example file
recommendations.rationale: Many environment variables with defaults; developers need clear reference
recommendations.suggested_change: Create .env.example showing all available configuration options

recommendations.recommendation: Add development makefile or shell script for common commands
recommendations.rationale: Long docker-compose commands with multiple -f flags are difficult to remember
recommendations.suggested_change: Create Makefile or docker-compose-helpers.sh with targets like 'make dev-up', 'make dev-logs'

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding backup/restore utilities for development data
recommendations.rationale: Volume management is good but no guidance for backing up/restoring local database state
recommendations.suggested_change: Add helper scripts in docker/ directory for database backup/restore workflows
